subject: IELTS Exam Preparation
description: Marking guidelines for IELTS practice responses across all four skills, based on official IELTS band descriptors.
importantNote: IELTS Writing and Speaking are assessed holistically against four equally-weighted criteria. Listening and Reading are assessed by the number of correct answers out of 40, converted to a band score.

writing: Task Achievement / Task Response (25%): Does the response fully address the task? Is the position clear and consistently maintained?
Coherence and Cohesion (25%): Is the response logically organised? Are cohesive devices used accurately and appropriately?
Lexical Resource (25%): Is a wide range of vocabulary used accurately? Are less common words and collocations used appropriately?
Grammatical Range and Accuracy (25%): Is a wide range of structures used? Are sentences mostly error-free?
speaking: Fluency and Coherence (25%): Does the candidate speak at length without unnatural pauses? Is speech well-organised?
Lexical Resource (25%): Is vocabulary varied and topic-appropriate? Are less common words and idiomatic language used?
Grammatical Range and Accuracy (25%): Is a variety of structures used? Are they mostly accurate?
Pronunciation (25%): Is speech intelligible? Is there appropriate use of stress, rhythm, and intonation?

rules.condition: Task Achievement/Response below Band 5 (major misunderstanding of task)
rules.marksAwarded: Caps overall Writing score at Band 5 regardless of other criteria

rules.condition: Under word count (fewer than 150 words Task 1 / 250 words Task 2)
rules.marksAwarded: Automatic penalty applied to Task Achievement/Response
Task Achievement capped at approximately Band 5

rules.condition: Introduction copied verbatim from the question
rules.marksAwarded: Those words not counted toward word count; Task Achievement penalised

rules.condition: Memorised or scripted response detected in Speaking
rules.marksAwarded: Fluency and Coherence score reduced; may be flagged for review

rules.condition: Bullet points not addressed in GT Task 1 letter
rules.marksAwarded: Task Achievement score reduced by approximately 1 band per missing bullet

writing.taskResponse: Addresses the task only partially; position may be unclear; ideas limited and not well supported
writing.cohesion: Some basic cohesive devices; may be inaccurate or repetitive
writing.lexical: Limited range; noticeable errors in word choice and spelling
writing.grammar: Limited range of structures; frequent errors; meaning sometimes obscured

speaking.fluency: Usually maintains flow but uses repetition or self-correction to keep talking; limited range of connectives
speaking.lexical: Has enough vocabulary for familiar topics; errors in less common vocabulary
speaking.grammar: Produces basic sentence forms reasonably accurately; limited complex structures
speaking.pronunciation: Generally intelligible; mispronounces some words; accent affects clarity occasionally

writing.taskResponse: Addresses all parts of the task, though some may be more fully covered; position generally clear
writing.cohesion: Coherent paragraphing; cohesive devices used but may be faulty or mechanical
writing.lexical: Adequate range; some awareness of style and collocation; some errors in word choice
writing.grammar: Mix of simple and complex structures; some errors in complex structures; generally clear meaning

speaking.fluency: Willing to speak at length; coherent but may have uneven flow; uses a range of connectives
speaking.lexical: Generally paraphrases successfully; less common vocabulary sometimes inaccurate
speaking.grammar: Mix of simple and complex structures; not always accurate but errors rarely cause misunderstanding
speaking.pronunciation: Generally intelligible; flexibly controls stress and intonation for some effect

writing.taskResponse: Addresses all parts of the task; clear position maintained throughout; main ideas extended and supported
writing.cohesion: Logically organised; range of cohesive devices used flexibly; clear referencing
writing.lexical: Sufficient range to allow flexibility and precision; uses less common items with some awareness of style; occasional inaccuracies
writing.grammar: Variety of complex structures; majority of sentences error-free; occasional errors in complex grammar

speaking.fluency: Speaks at length without noticeable effort; uses paraphrase effectively; coherently develops topics
speaking.lexical: Wide enough range to discuss most topics; uses idiomatic language naturally; occasional inaccuracies
speaking.grammar: Wide range of structures; frequently error-free; occasional slips; good control of complex structures
speaking.pronunciation: Easy to understand; uses a range of features with flexibility; only occasionally unclear

writing.taskResponse: Sufficiently addresses all requirements; well-developed position; well-chosen ideas; occasionally over-generalises
writing.cohesion: Sequences information and ideas expertly; manages paragraphing very well
writing.lexical: Wide range; fluent and flexible use; rare minor errors only
writing.grammar: Wide range; majority error-free; rare minor errors; flexible use in communication

speaking.fluency: Speaks fluently with only occasional repetition or self-correction; hesitation rare; coherent and connected discourse
speaking.lexical: Wide resource; flexible; precise in familiar and unfamiliar topics; effective circumlocution
speaking.grammar: Wide range; consistently accurate; only occasional slips
speaking.pronunciation: Wide range of features; precise; flexible; only occasional difficulty for the listener

bandDescriptors.description: All key features selected and highlighted; insightful overview; accurate and flexible data language; sophisticated comparisons; minimal errors; well above 150 words
Main features covered; clear overview present; good range of data language; accurate comparisons; occasional minor errors; well-organised
Key features covered but some may be underdeveloped; overview present but may be vague; adequate data language with some errors; generally organised
Task partially addressed; overview missing or unclear; limited data language; mechanical description; noticeable errors
Very limited coverage; no overview; very limited vocabulary; frequent errors obscure meaning
All requirements fully addressed; clear well-developed position; sophisticated ideas; wide range of vocabulary and complex structures; rare errors
All parts addressed; clear position maintained; well-developed ideas; flexible vocabulary; variety of structures; occasional errors
Most parts addressed; position generally clear; main ideas present but not fully extended; adequate vocabulary; mix of simple and complex structures
Partial coverage; position may be unclear; limited idea development; limited vocabulary; simple structures dominate; frequent errors
Limited response; position unclear or absent; very limited development; severe errors
All bullet points fully addressed; appropriate tone throughout; wide vocabulary range; sophisticated formal structures; minimal errors
All bullet points covered; register consistently appropriate; good vocabulary range; varied structures; occasional errors
All bullet points mentioned but some underdeveloped; register generally appropriate; adequate vocabulary; simple and some complex structures
Not all bullet points fully addressed; register occasionally inappropriate; limited vocabulary; errors noticeable
Bullet points missing or very underdeveloped; register often wrong; very limited vocabulary and structures
All bullets addressed fluently; sophisticated vocabulary; wide range of structures; natural pronunciation; nearly 2 minutes without effort
All bullets covered; good vocabulary range; variety of structures; minor errors; good fluency and pronunciation
Bullets generally covered; adequate vocabulary; some complex structures attempted; some hesitation; generally intelligible
Some bullets missed or very brief; limited vocabulary; mostly simple structures; noticeable pausing; some pronunciation errors
Very limited content; long pauses; very limited vocabulary; frequent errors; difficult to understand

IELTS-Q-RDG-001.scoringNote: 1 mark per correct TFNG answer. No negative marking.

bandEquivalence.5/5: Equivalent to Band 8+ performance on this question type
